revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|八幡宮来宮神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine | native_name = 八幡宮来宮神社 | image = The worship hall of Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine. Ito City, Shizuoka Prefecture.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.877222|139.096111}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Ōjin|ar|الإمبراطور أوجين|arz|الامبراطور اوجين|azb|اوجین (ایمپیراتور)|bn|সম্রাট ওজিন|cs|Ódžin|de|Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|es|Ōjin Tennō|eu|Ōjin enperadorea|fa|امپراتور اوجین|fi|Ōjin|fr|Ōjin|hr|Ōjin|id|Kaisar Ōjin|it|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|ka|ოძინი|ko|오진 천황|nl|Ojin|pl|Ōjin|pt|Ojin|ru|Император Одзин|sh|Car Ōjin|simple|Emperor Ōjin|sl|Cesar Odžin|sv|Ojin|th|จักรพรรดิโอจิง|tl|Emperador Ōjin|tr|Ōjin (imparator)|uk|Імператор Одзін|uz|Imperator Ōjin|vi|Thiên hoàng Ōjin|yo|Emperor Ōjin|zh_min_nan|Ôzin Thian-hông|zh_yue|應神天皇|zh|应神天皇|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine'''|八幡宮来宮神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11380552|ltq=Q11380552|qid=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 11 ** Series ordinal: 28 ** (Shimoda)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q134893133|ltq=Q134893133|qid=Q134893133|1=Sutou Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q134930730|ltq=Q134930730|qid=Q134930730|1=Sengen Shrine (Shimoda)}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shizuoka|lt=Kamo district|ar|مقاطعة كامو (شيزوكا)|ceb|Kamo-gun (distrito sa Hapon, Shizuoka-ken)|en|Kamo District, Shizuoka|fa|ناحیه کامو، شیزوئوکا|fr|District de Kamo (Shizuoka)|id|Distrik Kamo, Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|Q7311343|ltq=Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * はちまんぐうきのみやじんじゃ * イハレ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō * イハクラワケノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 256358406 == postal code (P281) == * 413-0232https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9080105004203 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 01199896 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039641|ltq=Q135039641|qid=Q135039641|1=Ihare- Shrine}} * {{ill|Ihakurawakeno- Shrine|lt=Ihakurawakeno- Shrine|qid=Q135039655|1=Ihakurawakeno- Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県伊東市八幡野1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡南伊豆町石廊崎', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:八幡宮来宮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11390944}} == Japanese content == {{混同|link1=Kinomiya Shrine (Izu City Hachiman)|Hatsuma Kinomiya Shrine (はつま きのみやじんじゃ)}} {{神社 |画像 = [[File:The worship hall of Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine. Ito City, Shizuoka Prefecture.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|38.9|N|139|5|47.2|E}}}} |名称=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|qid=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}} Yawatano 1 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 52 | 緯度秒 = 38.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 5 | 経度秒 = 47.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 | 地図 = Japan Shizuoka | ラベル位置 =left |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Iwakurawake-no-Mikoto |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} disputed shrine, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=tradition: {{ill|lt=Jingo-Keiun|Q1373049|qid=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 3 ({{ill|lt=769|Q30697|qid=Q30697|1=769}}) |本殿=two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=September 15|Q2846|qid=Q2846|1=September 15}} }} [[File:Torii of Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine. Ito City, Shizuoka Prefecture.jpg|thumb|270px|right|Torii]] '''Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine''' (はちまんぐうきのみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yawatano, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|qid=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a leading disputed shrine for the Shikinaisha "Iwakurawake-no-Mikoto Shrine." It is said to be a merger ({{ill|lt=gōshi|Q11412524|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}) of '''Kinomiya Shrine''' into the '''Hachimangū''' that originally stood at this location. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, each was separately designated as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, a rare example. === Shrine name === '''Hachimangū''' is said to be the origin of the place name "Yawatano," and '''Kinomiya Shrine''' was formerly also written as "Ki no Miya" (木宮, "tree shrine"). === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Honda Wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hachimangū) — said to have been the {{ill|lt=jinushi-gami|Q4160872|qid=Q4160872|1=Landlord deity}} (land deity) of this location from the beginning. *Iwakurawake-no-Mikoto (いわくらわけのみこと) (Kinomiya Shrine) — because originally enshrined in a rock cave, the name is thought to mean "deity of the {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} (sacred rock)." The deity was reputed to be an extreme lover of sake. The name "Ki (Tree) no Miya" of Kinomiya Shrine, legends of a drifting deity, and other features show strong characteristics of {{ill|lt=Kinomiya belief|Q11296898|qid=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, '''Hachimangū''' was invited (kanjō) in {{ill|lt=Jingo-Keiun|Q1373049|qid=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} 3 ({{ill|lt=769|Q30697|qid=Q30697|1=769}}) under a system of establishing one Hachimangū per province, and was designated as the Hachimangū of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Izu Province|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. '''Kinomiya Shrine''' is said to have drifted ashore in ancient times, carried on a jar, to a place called Kongōne-zu near Yawatano Port, east of the present location; it was enshrined in a rock cave called "Dō no Ana" and later transferred to Hachimangū. Furthermore, it is said that the two shrines were originally in separate halls, but during the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|qid=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era (782–806) they came to be enshrined together in a single main hall. Because Kinomiya Shrine's enshrined deity is Iwakurawake-no-Mikoto, it is identified as the Shikinaisha "Iwakurawake-no-Mikoto Shrine," and is also identified with the "Ju-shi-i-jō Iwakurawake-no-Myōjin" (Senior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade) recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Provincial Shrine Registry|Q11420490|qid=Q11420490|lt=Izu Provincial Deity Rank Record|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}' bearing a {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|qid=Q980266|1=Kōei}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|qid=Q6072|1=1343}}) colophon. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), '''Hachimangū''' was ranked as a gōsha, and in Meiji 9, '''Kinomiya Shrine''' was also elevated to gōsha. After the war, it belongs to the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Legends === *The deity of Kinomiya had such a great love of sake that when enshrined near the coast, he would demand sake offerings from ships passing offshore; the troubled people moved the deity inland to a corner of the current shrine grounds called "Motoyashiki," but the sea was still visible from there and the deity continued demanding sake, so he was moved a second time to beside the Hachimangū. *In the age of gods, when the deities gathered to divide the rivers, the Kinomiya deity fell asleep drunk and was left with only the muddy river of Naramato, where {{ill|lt=Atagawa Onsen|Q11569041|qid=Q11569041|1=Atagawa Onsen}} is located; the deity disliked it and refused to take it. This is said to explain why the Yawatano area is poorly supplied with water and has no hot springsKimura Hiroshi, "Kinomiya Shrine, Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine, Sugihoko-wake-no-Mikoto Shrine" (in 'Nihon no Kamigami — Jinja to Seichi'). === Rituals === *Annual festival ({{ill|lt=September 15|Q2846|qid=Q2846|1=September 15}}) — after the annual festival, the mikoshi processes to a temporary resting place (kariya) set up on the beach, stays one night, and returns the following {{ill|lt=September 16|Q2897|qid=Q2897|lt=16th|1=September 16}}. In former times, sacred sake was placed in bamboo tubes and sent to {{ill|lt=Ikonabime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11379183|qid=Q11379183|1=Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine}}. === Shrine buildings === *Honden (main hall) — two-bay, one-bay-deep {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-plate roofing, reconstructed in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|qid=Q7815|1=1795}}). Honda Wake-no-Mikoto is enshrined on the right (as viewed) and Iwakurawake-no-Mikoto on the left, creating a rare two-bay nagare-zukuri form. *Haiden (worship hall) — three-bay front, two-bay side, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with copper-plate roofing, with a one-bay {{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|qid=Q11415970|1=kōhai}} (front porch). Reconstructed in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|qid=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1824|Q6994|qid=Q6994|1=1824}}). The main hall and worship hall are connected by a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ryōsage-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} corridor hall (heiden); these three structures are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture. === Auxiliary shrines === Tenshō Kōtaijingū, Toyouke Kōtaijingū, Ōkuninushi Shrine, Wakamiya Hachimangū, Tenmangū, Ebisu Shrine, Koyasu Shrine, Akiba-gū, Hitaki Shrine, Sui Shrine === Cultural properties === === Natural monument (nationally designated) === *{{ill|lt=Yawatano Hachimangū/Kinomiya Shrine sacred grove|Q124367794|qid=Q124367794|1=Yawatano Hachimangū/Kinomiya Shrine sacred grove}} — a typical lucidophyll forest where tall trees such as {{ill|lt=Quercus salicina|Q4111865|qid=Q4111865|1=Quercus salicina}}, {{ill|lt=Quercus glauca|Q714124|qid=Q714124|1=Quercus glauca}}, {{ill|lt=Quercus gilva|Q3414213|qid=Q3414213|1=Quercus gilva}}, {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|qid=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}}, and {{ill|lt=Machilus thunbergii|Q5792073|qid=Q5792073|1=Machilus thunbergii}} are entwined with vines such as {{ill|lt=Uncaria rhynchophylla|Q1134307|qid=Q1134307|1=Uncaria rhynchophylla}}, with fern colonies below. In particular, the colony of {{ill|lt=Angiopteris lygodiifolia|Q15251683|qid=Q15251683|1=Angiopteris lygodiifolia}} is considered the northernmost natural habitat in the Japanese archipelago. Designated as a natural monument in 1934 (Shōwa 9). === Shizuoka Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties === *Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine Honden, Watadono, and Haiden === Itō City designated cultural properties === *Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine mikoshi (2 units) — repaired in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1778|Q7713|qid=Q7713|1=1778}}). *Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine yatai float === Other === Inside "Dō no Ana," the former site of Kinomiya Shrine, stand small shrines and stone Buddhist images called "{{ill|lt=Awashima-sama|Q1575740|qid=Q1575740|1=Awashima-no-Kami}}" and "{{ill|lt=Inari-sama|Q719665|qid=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}." The former Yawatano area consisted of two hamlets: the hillside "Oka" (agriculture-based) and the coastal "Hama" (fishing-based). Women of the Hama hamlet would seclude themselves in this cave during childbirth and menstruation, revering "Awashima-sama" as a deity of women's ailments and childbirth. Also, people of this area carried loads on their heads, a custom said to have been at nearly the northernmost limit on the {{ill|lt=Pacific|Q98|qid=Q98|1=Pacific Ocean}} coast of the {{ill|lt=Japanese archipelago|Q841337|qid=Q841337|1=Japanese archipelago}}. === Access === *{{ill|lt=Izukyū Line|Q864265|qid=Q864265|1=Izu Kyūkō Line}} {{ill|lt=Izu-Kōgen Station|Q6102186|qid=Q6102186|1=Izu-Kōgen Station}}, 15-minute walk === See also === {{Commonscat|Hachimangu Kinomiya jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Kinomiya Shrine|Q11523682|qid=Q11523682|1=Kinomiya Shrine}} * [https://travelog-jpn.blogspot.com/2011/04/12.html Chasing Kinomiya] === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol. 10: Izu Province / Kai Province. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press, 1981 *{{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ed. 'Nihon no Kamigami — Jinja to Seichi' vol. 10: Tōkai. {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, 1987 === External links === * [https://hatimanguukinomiya.net/ Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine] — official site {{Normdaten}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:伊東市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:伊東市の建築物]] [[Category:Test cases]]
